data.title: S&P 500, Nasdaq decline at the start of the second half of the year as the tech trade struggles
data.description:
Wall Street’s major indexes were mostly lower on Wednesday, with the Nasdaq Composite ((COMP:IND)) struggling to stay above water due to technology stocks declining amid ongoing volatility.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.1%, while the heavy-tech Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was -0.4%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

Among the worst decliners within tech were Micron (MU) -6.3%, Sandisk (SNDK) -8%, Nvidia (NVDA) -2.2%, and Broadcom (AVGO) -1.5%.

Meta Platforms (META), on the other hand, jumped more than 8.7% after Bloomberg reported it is building a cloud business for excess artificial intelligence compute.

On the economic side, the ISM Manufacturing PMI fell more than expected in June, while PMI Manufacturing unexpectedly dropped.

In addition, construction spending ticked up in May, slightly lower than expected, and the U.S. private sector added 98,000 jobs in June, missing the +117,000 consensus, according to ADP.

Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh said on Wednesday that the Fed will “chart a new course” on forward guidance, contrasting with his predecessor's practice of indicating which way policymakers were leaning in upcoming meetings.

Bond yields were mostly muted. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point lower at 4.46%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 5 basis points to 4.15%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) were lower at $68, while Brent (CO1:COM) was $71 per barrel.

data.title: AI infrastructure, chip stocks fall after report Meta building cloud business for excess AI compute
data.description:
Chip and AI infrastructure-related stocks fell on Wednesday after a report that Meta Platforms (META) is planning for a cloud infrastructure business to sell access to AI computing power and models.

Meanwhile, Wall Street averages were mixed. The tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) fell around 0.33%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) dipped about 0.03%. Meanwhile, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) rose about 0.22%.

Shares of Meta jumped about 8% following the report. Meanwhile, cloud computing leaders Amazon (AMZN), Microsoft (MSFT), and Alphabet (GOOG) (GOOGL) were largely in the green.

However, AI infrastructure solutions providers Nebius (NBIS) slumped nearly 12%, CoreWeave (CRWV) plummeted about 10%, and Super Micro Computer (SMCI) fell around 4% on Wednesday.

Shares of AI chip giant Nvidia (NVDA) fell about 2%. On Tuesday, the United States Office of Government Ethics released President Donald Trump's 2025 certified financial disclosure, which showed that during 2025, Trump owned  at least $5M in Nvidia, at least $1M in Palantir shares, and at least $5M in Apple. Meanwhile, Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) fell nearly 3%. Broadcom (AVGO) declined around 2%, while Qualcomm (QCOM) and Cerebras (CBRS) were largely flat. However, the second quarter saw big tech stocks gaining, with AMD surging 185.56%.

American memory giant Micron Technology (MU) slumped about 7% despite announcing it secured a long-term Strategic Customer Agreement with General Motors to supply the automaker with memory and storage platforms necessary for vehicle production. In the second quarter of 2026, Micron surged about 241%.

Meanwhile, Sandisk (SNDK) tumbled about 8% even as Bank of America upped its price target to $2,500 from $2,100, citing the growing supply-demand imbalance. Seagate Technology (STX) fell around 4%. Western Digital (WDC) declined nearly 5%.

Other AI and networking-related stocks were also largely in the red on Wednesday. Corning (GLW) plummeted about 10%, while Lumentum (LITE) slumped nearly 4%. Celestica (CLS), Coherent (COHR), and Ciena (CIEN) each fell around 3%, while Arista Networks (ANET) declined about 2%. Cisco (CSCO) and Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) each dipped about 1%.

Shares of Intel (INTC), Arm (ARM), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), and GlobalFoundries (GFS) each tumbled about 4%, while Marvell Technology (MRVL) fell nearly 3%. Lattice Semiconductor (LSCC) declined about 2%, while Analog Devices (ADI) was largely flat but in the red. However, Texas Instruments (TXN) was in the green.

Chip equipment makers: KLA (KLAC) tumbled about 8%, while Lam Research (LRCX) slumped nearly 7%. Applied Materials (AMAT) fell around 6%, while ASML (ASML) declined nearly 4% on Wednesday.

data.title: Crude inventory falls by 3.8M barrels for the week ended June 26 – EIA
data.description:
• Commercial crude stocks (excluding those in the Strategic Petroleum Reserve) for the week ended June 26: 408.4M barrels.
  • Crude inventory change: -3.8M barrels vs. -6.1M barrels for the week ended June 19.
  • Consensus estimate: -2.900M.
  • Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) -1% to $68.7/barrel.
  • ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (XLE).

data.title: Abbott gains as Baird issues new Outperform on growth outlook
data.description:
Shares of Abbott Laboratories (ABT) gained on Wednesday after Baird initiated coverage with an Outperform rating and a $121 price target, citing a stable growth trajectory for the MedTech company driven by a slew of upcoming catalysts.

Baird’s bullish view comes at a time when the Illinois-based healthcare giant has lost more than 25% this year, driven by two lackluster earnings reports, which most recently sent the stock to a 52-week low in April.

However, analyst David Rescott expects the company’s revenue and earnings to grow 6% - 8% or low double digits over the coming years consistently, driven by its medical devices and other segments.

“With a multi-year view, we think ABT's broader portfolio is well positioned to deliver stable ~6-8% top-line growth, especially as the company moves beyond near-term respiratory/China Diagnostic's headwinds,” Rescott wrote.

The analyst expects Abbott’s (ABT) consistent double-digit revenue and earnings growth to reemerge in 2027 and beyond as it overcomes the dilutive effects of its $21B acquisition of cancer diagnostics company Exact Sciences this year.

“We think ABT's catalysts over the next 12 months should position the company as an outperformer" among its large-cap MedTech peers, helping to generate investor interest in the overall sector, Rescott added.

data.title: Rheinmetall rises as Germany reportedly woos Trump with plan to make weapons for U.S.
data.description:
Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) up 5% in European trading Wednesday following a Financial Times report that Germany is seeking to partner with the U.S. to build more weapons domestically.

Other German defense companies also rose in Frankfurt, including Hensoldt (HAGHY) up 3.9%, Renk (RNKGF) up 3.8%, MTU Aero Engines (MTUAY) up 0.9%.

German officials are asking their U.S. counterparts to agree to a co-production deal ahead of nextweek’s NATO summit in Ankara, according to the report, in an effort by European countries to use their defense industrial capacity to steady their troubled relationship with the Trump administration.

The discussions reportedly have covered any items that could help both countries strengthen their defense capabilities, including co-production of long-range Tomahawk missiles and the advanced PAC-3 missiles used by Patriot air-defense systems.

Rheinmetall (RNMBF) (RNMBY) already produces fuselages for Lockheed Martin's F-35 fighter jets, and a new MBDA-Raytheon facility to make PAC-2 and GEM-T Patriot missiles is due to open later this year.

German officials say the country's large industrial base, including the struggling auto sector, means that a collaboration would offer a win-win solution for Europe and the U.S., and building critical U.S. weapons on German soil would help restore depleted U.S. defense stockpiles.

data.title: Amplitude initiated with Strong Buy rating at Raymond James
data.description:
• Amplitude (AMPL) was in focus on Wednesday as Raymond James initiated coverage on the AI analytics company with a Strong Buy rating and a $10 price target.
  • "We believe Amplitude is entering a multiyear period of growth acceleration with strong incremental margin expansion that should drive valuation multiple expansion" and an increase in margins, analysts at the firm wrote in a note to clients.

data.title: ISM Manufacturing PMI falls more than expected in June
data.description:
June U.S. ISM Manufacturing PMI: 53.3 vs. 53.9 consensus and 54.0 in May, according to data released by the Institute for Supply Management on Wednesday.

Developing... Check back for updates.

data.title: Construction spending ticks up in May, slightly lower than expected
data.description:
Construction spending edged up 0.1% M/M in May to $2,210.2B vs. +0.2% consensus and +0.3% prior (revised from +0.4%), according to data released by the U.S. Census Bureau.

That represents a 1.5% decline from May 2025. During the first five months of the year, construction spending added up to $858.4B, down 2.7% from the same period in 2025.

Developing… Check back for updates.

data.title: Most and least shorted mid- to mega-cap healthcare stocks at the end of June
data.description:
Short interest data from late June 2026 highlights deeply divided investor sentiment across the healthcare sector. Strong bearish bets remain heavily concentrated in speculative biotech plays like Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) and SELLAS Life Sciences Group (SLS), which carry short interests of 36.01% and 34.06%, respectively. Conversely, defensive pharma titans like Eli Lilly (LLY) and Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) maintain minimal short positions, both hovering near just 1%.

This caution aligns with the broader sector's performance, as healthcare remains a notable market laggard in 2026. Despite a robust 9.55% year-to-date rally in the S&P 500, the Health Care Select Sector SPDR® Fund (XLV) has posted a meager 2.49% gain—dragging on overall index performance despite its substantial 12.1% weighting in the benchmark.

Here are the top 5 most shorted mid- to mega-cap healthcare stocks (as a % of shares outstanding) as of June 30, 2026:

Intellia Therapeutics (NTLA) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 36.01%

SELLAS Life Sciences Group (SLS) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 34.06%

Hims & Hers Health (HIMS) – Health Care Services | Short Interest: 29.35%

Beam Therapeutics (BEAM) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 28.44%

Twist Bioscience Corporation (TWST) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 28.36%

Conversely, investors have steered clear of betting against established industry titans and specialized medical plays.

Here are the five least shorted mid- to mega-cap healthcare stocks (as a % of shares outstanding) as of June 30, 2026:

BeOne Medicines (ONC) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 0.81%

BrightSpring Health Services (BTSGU) – Health Care Services | Short Interest: 1.00%

Eli Lilly and Company (LLY) – Pharmaceuticals | Short Interest: 1.08%

BioNTech SE (BNTX) – Biotechnology | Short Interest: 1.08%

Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) – Pharmaceuticals | Short Interest: 1.11%

Health Care ETFs: (XLV), (VHT), (IHI), (IXJ), (IYH), (FHLC), (XPH), (PPH), (IBB), (IHE), (IBBQ) and (FXH).

data.description:
June S&P U.S. Manufacturing PMI: 53.9 vs. the initial estimate of 55.7 and 55.1 in May.
